title: Five Emirates ID perks that reach far beyond everyday checks
summary: Emirates ID: There are five perks and usages of the Emirates ID that take it beyond a form of identification. See them all here.
thbAction: Map the news to THB's provider CRM for referral growth, patient lifecycle engagement, and multi-facility relationship management across hospital and provider networks.
contentHtml:
Having an Emirates ID is mandatory for UAE citizens and residents, yet its role no longer ends at proof of identity.

That’s because banks, petrol stations, retailers, healthcare operators, airports and attractions have all attached live services to the card.

Cash access, station payments, patient registration, border processing and resident fares already sit inside that same identity system.

Residents often file the card alongside visa paperwork and compliance checks. Current service design across the UAE points to a broader operating role.

Here, Arabian Business outlines some of the card’s other perks.

Cash access without a debit card

Emirates NBD has built two separate routes around Emirates ID. Its cardless cash withdrawal service lets users generate a code in the mobile app and collect cash at an Emirates NBD ATM without a physical bank card.

Emirates NBD also says a cardless cash withdrawal carries no fee. Separate from that retail flow, Emirates NBD Interactive Teller Machines accept a valid Emirates ID for withdrawals up to AED 50,000.

Larger withdrawals require a cash cheque and must stay within the teller machine limit.

Mashreq has taken a similar path inside its self-service network. Mashreq says customers can use their Emirates ID to access all accounts at its ATMs. Those machines also handle statement requests, cheque book requests and mobile number updates.

Mashreq’s current terms for selected Neo accounts also record zero charges on local and international money transfers, ATM withdrawals and cheque book issuance under the bank’s stated conditions.

Bank rules still vary by account, yet the operating principle is already in place. An Emirates ID can replace plastic in several routine cash and account tasks.

Fuel, retail payments and station services

ADNOC Distribution has tied Emirates ID directly into its payment stack. Its app listing says ADNOC Wallet allows payments across the fuel station network using Emirates ID, RFID smart car tags, a mobile phone or ADNOC Plus.

ADNOC Distribution also positions its app as the route to faster fuelling, contactless payments and rewards across its services. Residents gain a practical alternative to carrying a wallet or bank card during short station visits.

Emirates ID linked to ADNOC Wallet keeps the transaction inside one system and cuts payment steps at the pump.

ADNOC’s reach extends beyond the nozzle. ADNOC Distribution promotes app-based offers across adjacent services, including car wash discounts and Oasis retail promotions.

That wider service mix matters because the card is no longer tied to fuel alone. One linked credential can handle forecourt payments, selected retail purchases and service offers inside the same station network.

For residents who refuel several times a month, that reduces transaction friction across a routine spend category.

Healthcare registration and insurance verification

Healthcare operators across the UAE already use Emirates ID as a primary registration document. SEHA’s public booking flow asks for an Emirates ID number alongside insurance type.

Cleveland Clinic Abu Dhabi asks residents to bring photo ID, preferably Emirates ID, and separately lists valid insurance cards in patient guidance.

Clinics still request the insurer card where required, so Emirates ID does not erase every paperwork step. Emirates ID does sit at the centre of intake and verification workflows.

That placement is what makes the card useful during routine doctor visits and specialist appointments.

Dubai Health Authority has also embedded resident identity inside its digital health channels. DHA says its app gives Dubai residents access to appointments, lab results and medications through one smart application.

Emirates ID remains one of the key credentials across those service layers. Patients still need to follow provider rules on insurance evidence and pre-approval. Emirates ID still does a large share of the heavy lifting before treatment begins.

Airport immigration

Dubai Airports says Smart Gates operate at DXB and DWC and move arriving and departing passengers through passport control within seconds.

GDRFA Dubai says UAE residents, UAE and GCC nationals, visa on arrival travellers and pre-issued visa holders can use the gates once registered.

GDRFA also runs an online inquiry tool so passengers can check Smart Gate registration status before travel. Frequent flyers gain the clearest benefit. Faster border processing cuts queue time during peak departures and dense arrival banks.

Emirates ID sits inside that wider smart travel architecture even as facial recognition and contactless processing have reduced the need to handle physical documents at every step.

UAE government guidance links Emirates ID with digital security and smart gate use at several airports. Dubai’s airport operator has already expanded the scale of automated processing across terminals, pushing more passengers into that fast lane model.

Residents who travel often are using an identity card that already reaches well beyond civil paperwork.

Hotel discounts and more

Leisure operators also use Emirates ID as proof of residency for local pricing. Visit Dubai lists a resident discount at Dubai Miracle Garden, where residents can present Emirates ID for a discounted AED60 ticket.

Visit Dubai terms for promotional activity also reserve the right to request Emirates ID, residency or passport documentation.

That commercial use matters because it shows the card working outside banking, healthcare and government processing. Emirates ID can also lower spend when resident rates are on the table.
